I have a Blazor Web App with Interactive Server (per-page).

I'm building a company intranet. I need ASP.NET Core Identity with
role-based access control. Here's what I need:

1. Set up Identity with three roles: Admin, Manager, and Employee.
   Use an in-memory database for now. Seed one test user per role
   on startup so I can test everything:
   - admin@company.com (Admin role)
   - manager@company.com (Manager role)
   - employee@company.com (Employee role)
   All with password "Test123!"

2. An interactive /admin/users page. Only Admins should be able to
   access this page. Show a list of all users with their roles
   (query from UserManager).

3. An interactive /reports page that both Admins and Managers can access
   but not regular Employees. Show some placeholder report content.

4. An interactive /directory page accessible to any authenticated user.
   Show a simple employee list.

5. The navigation menu should show or hide links based on the current
   user's roles — don't show the admin link to non-admins, don't show
   reports to employees.

6. If an unauthenticated user tries to visit any protected page, they
   should be sent to a login page. If an authenticated user visits a
   page they don't have the role for, they should see an "Access Denied"
   message.

data.reasoningText:
Now I have a good understanding of the codebase. Let me plan the implementation:

1. Add Identity packages and configure in-memory DB
2. Seed roles and users
3. Create login page (cookie-based auth with Identity)
4. Create the protected pages: /admin/users, /reports, /directory
5. Update NavMenu with role-based visibility
6. Configure authorization (redirect to login, access denied)

Let me implement this. Since this is a Blazor Server app with per-page interactivity, I need to be careful about how auth works. The auth state needs to flow from the server.

For Blazor Web App with Interactive Server render mode (per-page), I need:


- Entity Framework Core with Identity for managing users and roles
- An in-memory database for simplicity
- Cookie-based authentication configured
- The cascading authentication state to make auth available throughout the component tree

Let me start building this out.

data.content: Good — the Login page has no `@rendermode` so it will be SSR (static server rendering), which is correct since `SignInManager.PasswordSignInAsync` sets the auth cookie via `HttpContext`. The Logout page is also SSR. Let me also verify the Logout page doesn't have `@rendermode`:
Good — also SSR. The build is clean. Let me do a quick sanity check on the final Program.cs:

Everything looks good. Here's a summary of what was implemented:

**Files created:**
- `Data/ApplicationDbContext.cs` — Identity DbContext with in-memory EF Core
- `Data/SeedData.cs` — Seeds 3 roles + 3 test users on startup
- `Components/Account/Login.razor` — SSR login page with form POST
- `Components/Account/Logout.razor` — SSR logout page
- `Components/Account/RedirectToLogin.razor` — Redirects unauthenticated users to login
- `Components/Account/AccessDenied.razor` — "Access Denied" alert component
- `Components/Account/AccessDeniedPage.razor` — Standalone `/account/access-denied` page
- `Components/Pages/Admin/Users.razor` — Admin-only user list (`@attribute [Authorize(Roles = "Admin")]`)
- `Components/Pages/Reports.razor` — Admin+Manager reports page
- `Components/Pages/Directory.razor` — Any authenticated user

**Files modified:**
- `Program.cs` — Identity, in-memory DB, auth middleware, seed data
- `Components/Routes.razor` — `AuthorizeRouteView` with `<NotAuthorized>` handling
- `Components/Layout/MainLayout.razor` — Login/logout links in top bar
- `Components/Layout/NavMenu.razor` — Role-based `<AuthorizeView>` for nav links
- `Components/_Imports.razor` — Added auth-related usings
